page: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv6 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 236 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2156.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.35 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is comparatively limited, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression falling into the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth also shows constraints, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at 36.24 GB/sec. For lightweight AI workloads, the server achieves an average prompt processing speed of 436.99 tokens per second on a small 135M parameter model, though performance drops significantly on larger models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E4ds_v6 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The inclusion of a 236 GB local disk provides fast scratch space without requiring external storage attachments. This hardware profile is best suited for relational databases, caching layers, text processing applications, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 236 GB local disk. The server demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2156.0 and rapid single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as shown by lower stress-ng multi-core and uncached read bandwidth benchmarks. With an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio and built-in local storage, the E4ds_v6 provides a balanced, memory-dense profile. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, text processing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that do not demand heavy multi-threaded compute.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 236 GB local disk. Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, it is designed for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 236 GB local disk, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for memory-intensive workloads and database applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per-core ratio
Bundled 236 GB local storage disk with Premium Storage support
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression
Optimized for relational databases, caching, and memory-bound workloads
